accountingContextForTokenOf(uint256,addr.details: This surface is still synthetic for routing discovery, but it probes ERC-20 decimals when available and falls back to 18 if a token omits or breaks `decimals()`.

params.token: The address of the token to get accounting context for.

params.projectId: The project whose accounting contexts were requested.

params.accountingContexts: Ignored because this terminal derives accounting contexts at runtime.
params.projectId: The ID of the project whose accounting contexts would otherwise be configured.

params.amount: The amount of tokens to send.
params.memo: A memo to pass along to the emitted event.
params.projectId: The ID of the destination project.
params.shouldReturnHeldFees: Whether held fees should be returned based on the amount added.
params.token: The address of the token to pay in.

params.tokenB: The other token in the pair.

returns._0: bestLiquidity The highest liquidity found, or 0 if no pool exists.

params.buybackHook: The canonical buyback hook, deployed to the same address on each supported chain.
params.deployer: The address authorized to call `setChainSpecificConstants` exactly once. Held immutable so the constructor inputs are byte-identical across chains and the CREATE2 address is unified.
params.directory: A contract storing directories of terminals and controllers for each project.
params.tokens: A contract managing project token balances.
params.trustedForwarder: The trusted forwarder for the contract.

params.currency: The currency the caller wanted the surplus returned in.
params.decimals: The fixed-point precision the caller wanted the surplus returned in.
params.projectId: The project whose surplus was requested.
params.tokens: The token set the caller wanted surplus measured against.

returns.surplus: Always returns 0 because the router does not own project treasury balances.

discoverPool(address,address).details: V3-only by design: returns the deepest V3 pool whenever one exists, independent of whether a deeper V4 pool exists for the same pair. Reverts only when no V3 pool exists at all.

returns.pool: The V3 pool with the highest liquidity.

isTrustedForwarder(address).details: Indicates whether any particular address is the trusted forwarder.

params.projectId: The project whose balance migration was requested.
params.to: The destination terminal that would receive migrated funds.
params.token: The token whose balance migration was requested.

returns.balance: Always returns 0 because the router does not hold project balances.

pay(uint256,address,uint256,address,uint.details: Automatically handles direct forwarding, Uniswap swaps, JB token cashouts, or combinations.

params.amount: The amount of tokens to send.
params.beneficiary: The address to receive any tokens minted by the destination project.
params.memo: A memo to pass along to the emitted event.
params.minReturnedTokens: The minimum number of destination project tokens expected in return.
params.projectId: The ID of the destination project to pay.
params.token: The address of the token to pay with.

returns.beneficiaryTokenCount: The number of tokens minted for the beneficiary.

params.destProjectId: The destination project the router is trying to pay.
params.preferredToken: The token the cashout loop should prefer to land on, or `address(0)` for no preference.

returns.destTerminal: The terminal reached by the cashout loop, or address(0) if routing should continue.
returns.finalAmount: The amount of `finalToken` produced by the previewed cashout loop.
returns.finalToken: The token produced by the previewed cashout loop.

previewPayFor(uint256,address,uint256,ad.details: Returns the router's best estimate using current routing and quote data, including swap quotes when needed.

params.amount: The amount of the input token to provide.
params.beneficiary: The address that would receive any minted project tokens.
params.metadata: Extra data used to preview fund acceptance, routing, and the destination terminal call.
params.projectId: The ID of the destination project to pay.
params.token: The token to provide to the router.

returns.beneficiaryTokenCount: The number of project tokens that would be minted for the beneficiary.
returns.hookSpecifications: The pay hook specifications the resolved terminal would return.
returns.reservedTokenCount: The number of project tokens that would be reserved.
returns.ruleset: The current ruleset the destination terminal would use.

params.amount: The amount of `token` the destination terminal would receive.
params.beneficiary: The address to measure beneficiary token count for.
params.destTerminal: The terminal whose pay preview to query.
params.metadata: Metadata forwarded unchanged into the destination terminal preview.
params.projectId: The destination project that would receive the payment.
params.token: The token the destination terminal would receive.

returns._0: ruleset The ruleset returned by the destination terminal preview.
returns._1: beneficiaryTokenCount The beneficiary token count returned by the destination terminal preview.
returns._2: reservedTokenCount The reserved token count returned by the destination terminal preview.
returns._3: hookSpecifications The pay hook specifications returned by the destination terminal preview.

setChainSpecificConstants(address,addres.details: Callable only by `_DEPLOYER` and only once (when `wrappedNativeToken` is still `address(0)`). After this call all four values are effectively immutable for the contract's lifetime. Mirrors the `JBOptimismSuckerDeployer.setChainSpecificConstants` pattern so the contract's CREATE2 inputs stay byte-identical across chains and its deployed address is unified.

params.newFactory: The Uniswap V3 factory for pool discovery on this chain.
params.newPoolManager: The Uniswap V4 PoolManager on this chain (may be `address(0)` if V4 is not deployed there).
params.newUniv4Hook: The canonical Uniswap V4 router hook on this chain.
params.newWrappedNativeToken: The ERC-20 wrapper for the chain's native token (e.g. WETH on Ethereum, WCELO on Celo).

params.interfaceId: The ID of the interface to check for adherence to.

returns._0: A flag indicating whether the provided interface ID is supported.

trustedForwarder().details: Returns the address of the trusted forwarder.

uniswapV3SwapCallback(int256,int256,byte.details: Only the pool synchronously entered by `_executeV3Swap` can call this callback.

params.amount0Delta: The amount of token 0 used for the swap.
params.amount1Delta: The amount of token 1 used for the swap.
params.data: Data passed in by the swap operation: abi.encode(tokenIn).

BUYBACK_HOOK().notice: The canonical buyback hook whose preview hook specification metadata this router understands.

DEFAULT_TWAP_WINDOW().notice: The default TWAP window used for auto-discovered pools.

DIRECTORY().notice: The directory of terminals and controllers for projects.

MIN_TWAP_WINDOW().notice: The minimum acceptable TWAP window (2 minutes). Reverts if the pool's oldest observation is below this floor.

PERMIT2().notice: The Permit2 contract used for token approvals and transfers.

TOKENS().notice: Manages minting, burning, and balances of projects' tokens and token credits.

accountingContextForTokenOf(uint256,addr.notice: Returns a best-effort accounting context for any token the router can route.

accountingContextsOf(uint256).notice: Returns an empty array because this terminal accepts tokens dynamically rather than through a fixed accounting-context list.

addAccountingContextsFor(uint256,(addres.notice: Empty implementation to satisfy the interface. Accounting contexts are determined dynamically.

addToBalanceOf(uint256,address,uint256,b.notice: Add funds to a project's balance by routing the incoming token to whatever token the project accepts.

bestPoolLiquidityOf(address,address).notice: Return the highest discovered pool liquidity between two normalized tokens.

currentSurplusOf(uint256,address[],uint2.notice: This terminal holds no surplus because it routes funds onward instead of accounting for project balances itself.

discoverBestPool(address,address).notice: Discover the best pool across both V3 and V4 for a token pair.

discoverPool(address,address).notice: The best Uniswap V3 pool for a token pair, useful for off-chain queries.

factory().notice: The Uniswap V3 factory used for pool discovery and verification.

migrateBalanceOf(uint256,address,address.notice: Empty implementation to satisfy the interface. This terminal has no balance to migrate.

pay(uint256,address,uint256,address,uint.notice: Pay a project by routing the incoming token to whatever token the project accepts.

poolManager().notice: The Uniswap V4 PoolManager. Can be `address(0)` if V4 is not deployed on this chain.

previewCashOutLoopOf(uint256,address,uin.notice: Preview the recursive cashout loop the router would use for a project-token input.

previewPayFor(uint256,address,uint256,ad.notice: Preview a payment by simulating the router's routing logic in view context.

previewSwapAmountOutOf(address,address,u.notice: Preview the amount a direct token-to-token swap would return.

previewTerminalPayOf(address,uint256,add.notice: Preview a destination terminal payment from the router's caller context.

setChainSpecificConstants(address,addres.notice: One-shot setter for the chain-specific Uniswap and wrapped-native addresses.

supportsInterface(bytes4).notice: Indicates if this contract adheres to the specified interface.

uniswapV3SwapCallback(int256,int256,byte.notice: The Uniswap v3 pool callback where the token transfer is expected to happen.

univ4Hook().notice: The canonical Uniswap V4 router hook address used by supported hooked pools.

unlockCallback(bytes).notice: The Uniswap V4 unlock callback. Called by the PoolManager during `unlock()`.

wrappedNativeToken().notice: The ERC-20 wrapper for the native token.
